summary: Dialog has no website/statutes/published balance sheet, so documented institutional ties are thin — mostly member-employers (orgs whose execs attend) plus Thiel-network vehicles behind the 2026 Virginia campus. Thiel Foundation is the only Thiel entity with a sourced direct tie to Dialog-as-institution (campus build-out). Founders Fund + a16z documented as participant/ideological backers (NDCF paper), NOT formal funders. NATO Defense College Foundation is an OBSERVER (its analyst Spagnuolo published on Dialog), frequently mischaracterized as an affiliate. Thiel Capital/Fellowship/portfolio firms are a real corporate web but only INFERRED re: Dialog.

affiliates.tie_type: founder-linked vehicle backing Dialog's transition to a permanent Virginia campus
affiliates.evidence: Wikipedia + NDCF paper attribute Dialog's shift from nomadic to institutional phase / 2026 Virginia campus to the Thiel Foundation. Founded 2006, same year as Dialog.

affiliates.tie_type: participant / VC backer of 'dual-use' technologies discussed at Dialog
affiliates.evidence: NDCF paper names Founders Fund among VC funds whose luminaries 'underwrite the development of dual-use technologies' in Dialog's core. Thiel co-founded (2005).

affiliates.tie_type: participant / VC backer of 'dual-use' technologies discussed at Dialog
affiliates.evidence: NDCF paper names a16z among the venture funds whose luminaries underwrite dual-use tech at Dialog.

affiliates.tie_type: member-employer (CEO Alex Karp attends; Thiel is chairman)
affiliates.evidence: NDCF paper names Alex Karp as a core participant; Thiel is Palantir chairman.
NDCF paper names Palmer Luckey as nucleus of the 2025 Venice summit.

affiliates.tie_type: observer / analytical commentator (NOT a funder or host)
affiliates.evidence: Its analyst Gerardo Spagnuolo authored the paper analyzing Dialog. Observes Dialog; often mischaracterized as a tie.
NDCF paper names Larry Fink among attendees. Employer-of-attendee only.
Oligarch Watch/Forbes list Gen. Grynkewich among attendees.

affiliates.tie_type: member-employer (Tom Lue, Neal Mohan among attendees)
affiliates.evidence: Forbes lists DeepMind's Tom Lue and YouTube CEO Neal Mohan among leaked attendees.

affiliates.tie_type: member-employer (Peter Goettler, Ryan Stowers) — libertarian ideological overlap
affiliates.evidence: Forbes lists Cato head Goettler and Koch Foundation's Stowers; consistent with 'heavily libertarian-leaning' character.

affiliates.tie_type: Thiel family-office hub (incubated Founders Fund et al.) — network tie, no documented direct Dialog role
affiliates.evidence: Incubator of Founders Fund, Mithril, Valar, Thiel Fellowship. No source ties it directly to Dialog; inferred via Thiel only.

affiliates.tie_type: Thiel Foundation program — network tie, no documented direct Dialog role
affiliates.evidence: No source links it to Dialog events/funding; connection only via shared Thiel control.

affiliates.entity: Founders Fund portfolio firms (SpaceX, OpenAI, Stripe)
affiliates.tie_type: co-investment network — no documented direct Dialog tie beyond individual attendees (e.g. OpenAI's Greg Brockman)
affiliates.evidence: Some executives attend individually; firms themselves have no documented institutional Dialog tie.
MHL owns Powerscourt Estate, reserved for Dialog's 2026 retreat (later cancelled).

affiliates.entity: San Clemente Palace Kempinski (Venice), Ritz-Carlton Dove Mountain, Bacara/Ritz-Carlton Santa Barbara, Sundance Resort
affiliates.evidence: Forbes + NDCF document these as hosts, incl. 2025 Venice summit.

affiliates.entity: Bilderberg Group / Bohemian Club / World Economic Forum
affiliates.evidence: Invoked as structural parallels ('Bilderberg meets Silicon Valley'); no organizational affiliation.
